title: What Meditation Is
body:
Meditation is a family of practices that train attention, awareness, and emotional regulation. Some forms focus on the breath or a mantra. Others emphasise open awareness, loving-kindness, or systematic observation of bodily sensation. The common thread is deliberate mental training.

Historically, meditation comes from long contemplative traditions, especially Buddhist, Hindu, Taoist, and Christian practices. Modern secular meditation usually strips away the religious frame while keeping the core method intact: repeatedly noticing where the mind is and gently returning it.

The point is not to stop thinking. It is to develop a better relationship with thought. Over time, practitioners often become more aware of distraction, reactivity, and habitual mental loops, which makes them easier to interrupt.
keyPoints: Meditation is mental training, not just relaxation
It includes attention practices, open awareness, and compassion practices
Traditional and secular forms share a common core
The skill being trained is meta-awareness
Meditation helps people notice and redirect attention
It is best understood as practice, not belief
furtherReading: 'Wherever You Go, There You Are' by Jon Kabat-Zinn
'Altered Traits' by Daniel Goleman and Richard Davidson
